{{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
[[Empress Jingū]]
Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== [[ja:本住吉神社]] '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

|website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] {{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
[[Empress Jingū]]
Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== [[ja:本住吉神社]] '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:shinto {{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto

Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== [[ja:本住吉神社]] '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== [[ja:本住吉神社]] '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}{{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}{{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}{{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

{{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
[[Empress Jingū]]
Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

{{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
[[Empress Jingū]]
Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] }} [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

{{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto

Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[Category:remove from Pages of uncertain originality]][[Category:]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] }} [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[Category:remove from Pages of uncertain originality]][[Category:]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] }} [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[Category:]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] }} [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}
