[[Empress Jingū]] |rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}}

{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=本住吉神社 | 所在地=[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[東灘区]]住吉宮町7丁目1-2 | 位置={{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=本住吉神社}} | 祭神=[[住吉三神|底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命]]
[[神功皇后]] | 社格=県社 | 例祭=[[5月13日]] | 画像=[[ファイル:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] | 地図={{Location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=本住吉神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''本住吉神社'''（もとすみよしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[東灘区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[住吉三神]]（底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命）および[[神功皇后]]を主[[祭神]]とし、[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見命]]を配祀する。 東灘区渦森台北端の西山谷（住吉川の支流）西岸に奥宮（[[1970年]]建立 主神：住吉三神 配神：神功皇后・[[宗像三女神]]・[[厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)|厳島大神]]（宗像三女神と同じ）・[[オオヤマツミ]]）がある。 ==歴史== 社伝では、[[日本書紀]]において、神功皇后の[[三韓征伐]]からの帰途に船が進まなくなり、神託により住吉三神を祀ったと記される「大津渟中倉之長峡（おおつのぬなくらのながお）」の地が当地であり、当社が住吉三神鎮祭の根源であると伝え、そのために古くから「本住吉」と呼ばれるとしている。「大津渟中倉之長峡」の地は現在の[[住吉大社]]であるとする説が有力であるが、当社では住吉大社も当社からの[[分霊|勧請]]であるとしている。[[本居宣長]]も本住吉神社の主張を支持している。 [[延喜式神名帳]]には記載されていない。古くから「本住吉」と称してきたが、明治初年の『神社調査明細書』には「住吉神社」と記載された。神社側からの申請により、[[1908年]]（[[明治]]38年）に「本住吉神社」に復称した。社殿は[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）の[[阪神・淡路大震災]]で大きな被害を受け、現在の社殿はその後復興したものである。 ==祭事== [[5月4日]]・[[5月5日|5日]]には境内の車庫に保管されている8台の[[地車]]（山田・呉田・住之江・空・西・吉田・茶屋・反高林）の巡行が行われる。 ==住吉村道路元標== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|住吉村道路元標]] 境内参道西脇にある。但し、[[住吉村 (兵庫県)|住吉村]]道路元標として現役であった頃から現地にあったか否かは不明。 本住吉神社境内の南に接して[[西国街道]]（現[[国道2号]]）が東西に走り、東に接して有馬道（別名[[有馬街道]]・住吉有馬道）が南北に走る。 == 境内 == 画像:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|正面より 画像:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|拝殿 画像:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|本殿 画像:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|境内 画像:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|境内 画像:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|境内 画像:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|境内 画像:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|境内 画像:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|境内 ==その他== [[2020年]]（[[令和]]2年）、境内に[[ヴィッセル神戸]]の[[アンドレス・イニエスタ]]に関する「イニエスタ来日の碑」という石碑が建立された。碑文は歌人の[[屋繁男]]の短歌が彫られている{{Cite web|和書|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html 本住吉神社]（兵庫県神社庁サイト） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もとすみよししんしや}} [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:東灘区の歴史]] [[Category:東灘区の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=本住吉神社 | 所在地=[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[東灘区]]住吉宮町7丁目1-2 | 位置={{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=本住吉神社}} | 祭神=[[住吉三神|底筒男命

{{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
[[Empress Jingū]]
Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:]] }} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] }} [[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{citation needed|Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages whose categories were destroyed by the bot]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

[[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

[[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]] {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Qq]][[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

[[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

[[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[Category:c]][[Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 1]] {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:c]][[Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] }} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyogo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 1]] {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]][[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto
Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyōgo-Jinjacho] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Motosumiyoshi Shrine |location = [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Higashinada Ward]], Sumiyoshi Miya-machi 7-chome 1-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|43|07|N|135|15|36.5|E|scale:30000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Motosumiyoshi Shrine}} |deity = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto
Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto]]
[[Empress Jingū]] |Rank = Prefectural Shrine |festival = [[May 13]] |image = [[File:Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG|250px]] |map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=07|lon_deg=135|lon_min=15|lon_sec=36.5|label=Motosumiyoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} is a Japanese [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe|Higashinada]] ward, [[Kobe]].[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyōgo-Jinjacho] It is one of the biggest shrines in western Kobe. It is next to [[Sumiyoshi Station (JR West)|Sumiyoshi Station]]. There is documentary evidence that the shrine has existed since the 13th century.{{cite web|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html |title=本住吉神社 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine holds a [[danjiri]] festival annually in May.{{cite web|url=http://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/ward/kuyakusho/higashinada/foreign/overview/danjiri_festival.html |title=Higashinada Ward Danjiri festival |date=25 March 2008 |access-date=10 May 2016}} Portable shrines are wheeled through neighborhoods around the shrine by teams of about 50 people. ==Overview== '''Motosumiyoshi Shrine''' (もとすみよしじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Higashinada Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The main deities enshrined are the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto, Uwatsutsu-no-O no Mikoto) and [[Empress Jingū]], with additional enshrinements of [[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto]] and [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]]. At the northern end of Uzu Moridai in Higashinada Ward, there is an Okumiya (inner shrine) established in [[1970]], dedicated to the Sumiyoshi Sanjin and enshrining Empress Jingū, the [[Munakata Three Goddesses]], and [[Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Ōkami]] (same as Munakata Three Goddesses), and [[Ōyamatsumi]]. ==History== According to the shrine's tradition, the "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]], where the ship stopped on Empress Jingū's return from the [[Three Korean Campaigns]], and where the Sumiyoshi Sanjin were enshrined, is said to be this location. The shrine claims to be the original enshrinement place of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, hence the name "Motosumiyoshi." Although there is a strong belief that "Ōtsu Nunakakura no Nagasa" refers to the current location of [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]], Motosumiyoshi Shrine asserts that Sumiyoshi Taisha was derived from its own enshrinement. [[Motoori Norinaga]] also supported Motosumiyoshi Shrine's claim. It is not listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Although it has been called "Motosumiyoshi" for a long time, the shrine was listed as "Sumiyoshi Shrine" in the early Meiji period 'Shrine Survey Details'. Upon the shrine's request, the name was officially restored to "Motosumiyoshi Shrine" in [[1908]]. The shrine buildings suffered significant damage during the [[Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake]] in [[1995]], and the current shrine buildings were reconstructed afterward. ==festivals== On [[May 4]] and [[May 5]], eight [[dashi]] floats (Yamada, Kureta, Suminoe, Sora, Nishi, Yoshida, Chaya, and Soko-Rindo) stored in the precincts are paraded. ==Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard== [[File:本住吉神社 Z6A 5206 C01.jpg|thumb|center|160px|Sumiyoshi Village Road Original Standard]] Located on the west side of the shrine's precincts. It is unclear whether it was there when it was in use as the original road standard of [[Sumiyoshi Village]]. The [[Saigoku Kaido]] (currently [[National Route 2]]) runs east-west, adjacent to the south of the shrine precincts, and the Arima Road (also known as [[Arima Kaido]] or Sumiyoshi Arima Road) runs north-south adjacent to the east. == Grounds == File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4X 9003 C01.jpg|View from the front File:本住吉神社の社殿 - D4X 9011 C01.jpg|Haiden File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2617 C01.jpg|main hall File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9885 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - D4X 9883 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社の境内 - D4A 0151 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2613 C01.jpg|Grounds File:本住吉神社 - DF3 2614 C01.jpg|Grounds File:DF3_2615_C01.jpg|Grounds ==Other== In [[2020]] (Reiwa 2), a stone monument dedicated to [[Andrés Iniesta]] of [[Vissel Kobe]], called the "Iniesta Monument," was erected on the shrine grounds. The monument is inscribed with a tanka by poet [[Ya Shigeo]]{{Cite web|title=「もう歴史上の偉人やん！」神戸の神社にイニエスタの石碑 「われらに伝えるサッカーは…」石に刻んだ思い、建立者に聞いた｜まいどなニュース |url=https://maidonanews.jp/article/14917509 |website=まいどなニュース |date=2023-05-26 |access-date=2023-05-27 |language=ja-JP}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Motosumiyoshi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency site) {{shinto-stub}} == Controversy == It is insisted by the Shrine in its "Chronicle of Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine" (2000), based on [[Kojiki-den]] written by [[Motoori Norinaga]], that the head of Sumiyoshi Shrine originally moved from this Shrine to [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]] because Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine has "Moto-" which means "the Head".[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301005.html Hyōgo Jinjacho][http://kouinequal.arrow.jp/sumiyosijinjya/kobe/koubesumiyosi.html Azumi-zoku and Sumiyoshi Shrine-6] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Sumiyoshi Faith}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 1]] {{translated page|ja|本住吉神社|version=101466589|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashinada Ward]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to empress Jingū]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[en:Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] [[ja:本住吉神社]]

{{short description|Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|本住吉神社|Motosumiyoshi Jinja}} | image = Motosumiyoshi-Jinja 002.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.7187453|135.260127|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:20000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto
